behavior_summary: U8P_TAPESTEW::look runs when the tapestry item is examined. It sets an item info key (0x0208) and applies a process exclusion step to adjust the item's processing/visibility state, then asks the item/process to 'bark' the literal short description string 'tapestry ' to the viewer. The bark return value is captured and a small implies/pop sequence is executed before the process suspends and returns.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TAPESTEW::look: set_info(0x0208) -> process_exclude -> push literal 'tapestry ' -> Item::bark(item_ptr, 'tapestry ') -> capture retval -> implies/pop -> suspend/return
open_questions: The concrete semantics of info id 0x0208 (what game state or UI behavior it toggles) are not explicit and are inferred from common patterns.
The exact target/viewer PID for Item::bark (whether the bark is emitted by the item to the player process or routed differently) is only inferred from usage patterns.
The purpose and effect of the implies/pop sequence after the bark return value is captured is unclear (likely a small control/return-value handling idiom).

main_function.name: U8P_TAPESTEW::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's examine/U8P_TAPESTEW::look action on the tapestry: prepare item state and present the short description/name ('tapestry ') via the item bark routine, then suspend.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player (or relevant actor/process) examines or looks at the tapestry item.

function_map.name: U8P_TAPESTEW::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_TAPESTEW::look event handler for the tapestry item; prepares item info/state and invokes presentation (Item::bark).

external_calls.purpose: Request the item/process to emit a short description message ('tapestry ') to the appropriate target/viewer.
Engine operation that sets info key 0x0208 on the referenced object; used to change item state/metadata prior to barking (inferred).
Engine operation to exclude or adjust processing for the object (likely to prevent further automatic processing while barking/displaying).
Suspend the current process after completing the bark and cleanup sequence.
